Race screen — 2026-07-07 · updated 15:21

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 2/19 (11%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 2/19 (11%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-06 · updated 09:02

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 9/21 (43%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 8/21 (38%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-05 · updated 09:11

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 15/18 (83%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 7/18 (39%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-04 · updated 09:02

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 21/34 (62%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 20/34 (59%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-03 · updated 09:04

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 13/25 (52%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 10/25 (40%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-02 · updated 09:02

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 12/23 (52%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 10/23 (43%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-01 · updated 09:01

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 10/21 (48%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 6/21 (29%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-30 · updated 09:04

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 15/22 (68%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 11/22 (50%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-29 · updated 09:02

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 15/23 (65%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 15/23 (65%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-28 · updated 09:01

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 10/15 (67%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 7/15 (47%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-27 · updated 09:01

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 21/33 (64%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 19/33 (58%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-26 · updated 21:11

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 18/30 (60%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 15/30 (50%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-25 · updated 09:01

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 16/22 (73%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 11/22 (50%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-06-24 · updated 09:00

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 3/5 (60%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 3/5 (60%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded
